data.title: Saga: SagaEVM experienced a security incident and has been suspended; approximately $7 million in assets were transferred to the Ethereum mainnet.
data.description: Saga issued an announcement stating that a security incident occurred on SagaEVM on January 21st. The chain has been suspended at block height 6,593,800, and an investigation and mitigation effort is underway. Saga stated that the incident involved a series of contract deployments, cross-chain operations, and subsequent liquidity transfers. For security reasons, the team will maintain the SagaEVM suspension until the investigation and mitigation measures are complete. Current priorities include: preventing further impact, verifying the scope of impact through archived data and execution traces, hardening relevant components before restarting, and only disclosing confirmed facts. Regarding the scope of impact, the affected entities are the SagaEVM chainlet and its components Colt and Mustang; unaffected entities include the Saga SSC mainnet, Saga protocol consensus, validator security, and other Saga chainlets. The announcement also stated that approximately $7 million worth of USDC, yUSD, ETH, and tBTC were transferred to the Ethereum mainnet. The attacking address has been identified, and Saga is working with exchanges and cross-chain bridges to blacklist the address and attempt to recover the assets. Saga emphasizes that there was no consensus failure, validator compromise, or signature key leakage in this incident, and the overall Saga network structure remains secure. The team will release a full technical recap report after confirming the root cause, fixing and hardening the affected components.

data.title: Argentine fintech company Pomelo has raised $55 million in Series C funding, led by investors including Kaszek.
data.description: Argentine fintech company Pomelo announced the completion of a $55 million Series C funding round. The round was led by Kaszek and Insight Partners, with participation from Index Ventures, Adams Street Partners, S32, Endeavor Catalyst, Monashees, and TQ Ventures. Pomelo stated that the funds will primarily be used to expand its credit processing operations in its two core markets of Mexico and Brazil, and to support the launch of a credit card product denominated in a global stablecoin. (Bloomberg)

data.title: River briefly broke through 45 USDT, with a 24-hour increase of nearly 30%.
data.description: According to GMGN data, River briefly broke through 45 USDT and is currently trading at 46.14 USDT, a 24-hour increase of 29.81%. Previously, it was reported that Justin Sun invested $8 million in River to support its deployment of chain-abstract stablecoin infrastructure within the TRON ecosystem.

data.title: U.S. Stock Markets See Significant Gains
data.description: The Dow Jones Industrial Average closed with a rise of 588.64 points, or 1.21%, reaching 49,077.23 points on January 21. According to ChainCatcher, the S&P 500 Index increased by 78.76 points, or 1.16%, closing at 6,875.62 points. Meanwhile, the Nasdaq Composite Index gained 270.5 points, or 1.18%, ending at 23,224.82 points.

data.title: Eric Balchunas Questions US Equity Bears' Perspective
data.description: Eric Balchunas, senior ETF Analyst at Bloomberg Intelligence, posted on X questioning the rationale behind the pessimistic outlook of U.S. equity bears. He expressed confusion over their negative stance towards American equities, suggesting that their perspective might not align with the current market conditions. Balchunas implied that while everyone is entitled to their own opinion, the bearish sentiment towards U.S. equities seems unfounded given the prevailing economic indicators.

data.title: Spot ETF Data Disappoints Amid Speculative Investments
data.description:
Phyrex posted on X. On Tuesday, the spot ETF data fell short of expectations, a development that was anticipated. Many traditional investors in the ETF sector are engaging in speculative behavior, investing not out of confidence in Bitcoin's future, but rather in the hope of price increases. Consequently, when prices decline, these investors are willing to exit even at a loss.

In a notable development, at 3:27 AM, U.S. President Donald Trump announced the suspension of tariffs related to Greenland, leading to an immediate rebound in risk market valuations. Both the U.S. stock market and Bitcoin responded positively to this news, suggesting that Wednesday's data might not be as disappointing. However, the trend of investors chasing rising prices and selling during declines persists.

data.title: Tom Lee Predicts Potential Market Turbulence in Coming Weeks
data.description: BitMine Chair Tom Lee posted on X about potential market turbulence in the upcoming weeks. He highlighted three main reasons for this forecast. Lee's insights are closely followed by investors and analysts, as he is known for his market predictions. The details of his analysis were shared on social media, drawing attention from various financial circles. Lee's comments come at a time when markets are closely monitoring economic indicators and geopolitical developments. His perspective adds to the ongoing discussions about market stability and potential volatility.

data.title: Delphi Digital: Based on market pricing patterns for volatility, the market is more likely to rise in the near future.
data.description: Delphi Digital published an article on X stating that fundamental data suggests the market may be inaccurately pricing volatility currently. When implied volatility trades significantly below its fair value while Bitcoin is at historical highs, a significant pullback typically follows. The most recent such signal was triggered last October, just four days before a large-scale liquidation event. Conversely, historically, periods when implied volatility is extremely overvalued relative to its fair value have often presented excellent buying opportunities. Multiple leading indicators suggest that current volatility is likely mispriced by the market, and its future trend leans towards the upside.

data.title: Seeker (SKR) surged over 460% intraday, pushing its market capitalization above $440 million.
data.description: According to GMGN data, Seeker (SKR), a native asset of the Solana Mobile ecosystem, saw its intraday increase exceed 460%, with its market value rapidly rising and once surpassing $440 million. It is currently trading at $430 million.

data.title: Pomelo Secures $55 Million in Series C Funding to Expand Credit Processing in Latin America
data.description: Argentinian fintech company Pomelo has successfully raised $55 million in a Series C funding round. According to Foresight News, the round was led by Kaszek and Insight Partners, with participation from Index Ventures, Adams Street Partners, S32, Endeavor Catalyst, Monashees, and TQ Ventures. The funds will be utilized to expand Pomelo's credit processing operations in Mexico and Brazil, its two key markets, and to support the launch of credit cards denominated in global stablecoins.

data.title: BitGo Sets IPO Price at $18 Per Share, Surpassing Initial Range
data.description: BitGo, a cryptocurrency custody firm, has set its initial public offering (IPO) price at $18 per share, exceeding the previously anticipated range of $15 to $17. According to PANews, the company, along with existing shareholders, is selling a total of 11.8 million shares, aiming to raise approximately $213 million. This valuation places BitGo's market worth at over $2 billion based on the current issued share capital. The IPO is underwritten by Goldman Sachs and Citigroup, and BitGo is scheduled to list on the New York Stock Exchange this Thursday under the ticker symbol 'BTGO.' This marks the first IPO by a cryptocurrency company in 2026.

data.title: Solana's Space Platform Faces Controversy Over Fundraising Exceeding Initial Goal
data.description:
Solana's leverage prediction market platform, Space, has sparked controversy following its public fundraising campaign. According to Odaily, the project initially aimed to raise $2.5 million but ended up collecting $20 million. The team announced plans to return $7.3 million while retaining the excess funds, explaining that the $2.5 million target was a 'soft cap' rather than a 'hard cap.'

The project further argued that $2.5 million would only sustain operations for a few months and represented the minimum amount needed for initial delivery. They emphasized that this sum is insufficient for the long-term development of the leverage prediction market infrastructure.

data.title: Hashed launches Maroo concept, a Layer 1 blockchain targeting the Korean Won stablecoin economy.
data.description: Hashed Open Finance, a subsidiary of crypto venture capital firm Hashed, released a lightweight white paper proposing a new Layer 1 blockchain concept called Maroo, positioned to serve the Korean Won (KRW) stablecoin economy. Maroo is designed as a "sovereign blockchain" that balances the openness of public chains with financial regulatory compliance, emphasizing financial-grade features such as auditability and privacy protection. Transaction fees will be paid in KRW stablecoins to reduce volatility and increase user engagement. Hashed Open Finance points out that existing public chains (such as Ethereum) struggle to implement anti-money laundering and KYC mechanisms at the network level, and their highly transparent designs may expose sensitive personal and corporate information. To address this, Maroo introduces a "dual-track" architecture: an "open path" allows free wallet creation and transactions, while a "compliance path" introduces identity verification and restrictions based on transaction size or scenario. Simultaneously, its programmable compliance layer (PCL) can automatically perform quota and sanctions reviews during transactions and supports updates as regulations change; a verifiable privacy framework enables selective information disclosure under necessary legal procedures. Furthermore, Maroo has reserved AI integration capabilities for authenticating AI agents and managing their permissions and spending limits. Hashed CEO Simon Kim stated that stablecoins are becoming an important part of the global financial infrastructure, and Maroo aims to explore an open technology path that conforms to international standards, while respecting the South Korean regulatory environment, to provide a foundation for experimenting with next-generation financial services for banks, financial institutions, and fintech companies. The report points out that this plan aligns with South Korea's direction in advancing KRW stablecoin legislation, and the relevant rules are expected to be incorporated into the currently drafting "Digital Asset Basic Law," potentially being finalized in the first quarter of this year. (The Block)

data.title: Solana co-founder Toly: Optimal Capital Formation for Early-Stage Startups May Follow a Three-Step Approach
data.description: Solana co-founder Toly stated that if this model proves effective, he believes the optimal approach for early-stage startups in terms of capital formation likely includes three points: first, providing a staking mechanism for long-term holders; second, releasing more than 20% of the tokens on the TGE day; and third, ideally avoiding the introduction of investors, or if investors are involved, unlocking 100% of the tokens in a single transaction on the same day one year after the TGE. He indicated that the remaining outcomes will depend on Product-Market Fit (PMF).

data.title: Sources: BitGo IPO pricing raised to $18 per share
data.description: Crypto asset custody company BitGo has set its initial public offering (IPO) price at $18 per share, higher than its previous target range of $15 to $17. In this offering, BitGo and some existing shareholders will sell approximately 11.8 million shares. The arrangements are still under discussion, and specific details are subject to change. (Bloomberg)

data.title: Space's prediction market IPO sparks controversy as team retains tens of millions of dollars in oversubscribed funds, citing a "soft cap."
data.description: The public offering of Space, a leveraged prediction market platform within the Solana ecosystem, has sparked controversy. The project, originally planned to raise $2.5 million, actually raised $20 million. The team subsequently announced it would return $7.3 million and retain the remaining oversubscribed funds, arguing that the $2.5 million was a "soft cap" rather than a "hard cap." The project further argued that "$2.5 million can only sustain operations for a few months; this amount represents only the minimum required for initial shipments and is insufficient to support the construction of leveraged prediction market infrastructure over many years."

data.description:

data.title: Neynar's Acquisition of Farcaster Sparks Positive Remarks from Mask Founder
data.description:
Mask founder Suji Yan commented on Neynar's acquisition of Farcaster, stating that increased competition is beneficial. According to Odaily, Yan mentioned hearing rumors earlier but did not verify them with the Neynar team. He emphasized the importance of collaboration and congratulated the Neynar team, disclosing his status as a minor shareholder and user of Neynar's software and technology, praising the team's excellence.

In related news, Aave founder Stani announced that the management of the social protocol Lens will be transferred to the Mask team, with the original team serving as technical advisors.
